architecture_summary: Extend the existing Personal Assistant dry-run packet into a no-effect proof packet for inbox, task-intake, calendar-conflict, draft-response, approval-request, no-send, and memory-candidate flow closure.
data_model_changes: Increase dry-run source artifacts from nine to eleven by adding calendar request and planning projection refs.
Increase dry-run stages from ten to twelve by adding calendar_conflict_reasoning and task_intake_projection.
Add explicit closure flags for calendar conflict, task intake, draft response, approval request, no-send proof, and memory-admission candidate review.
Add no-effect boundary fields for external send, mailbox mutation, calendar write, and task write.
Refresh the dry-run packet fixture and dependent foundation closure packet fixture.
implementation_plan: Patch dry-run collector topology and no-effect boundary.
Patch dry-run schema constants and closure requirements.
Patch dry-run validator closure and topology checks.
Regenerate dry-run and foundation closure fixtures.
Add focused tests for the expanded calendar/task/memory/no-send proof path.
Run focused validators, focused pytest, protocol manifest validation, SDLC artifact validation, schema validation, and whitespace checks.
invariants: The packet is not execution authority.
The packet is not connector authority.
The packet is not memory admission.
No email send, calendar write, task write, mailbox mutation, connector mutation, memory write, or system-of-record write is performed.
Foundation closure remains digest-bound and no-effect.
rollback_plan: Restore dry-run source count to nine and stage count to ten.
Remove calendar-conflict and task-intake dry-run stages and closure flags.
Restore the previous dry-run packet and foundation closure packet fixtures.
Remove the SDLC requirement and design artifacts.
tradeoffs: The packet proves assistant flow composition without exercising live providers.
Live Gmail, Calendar, task-system, and memory writes remain separate authority threads.
